title: To The High And Noble Prince George, Duke, Marquis, And Earl Of Buckingham.
content:
Never my book's perfection did appear
    Till I had got the name of Villars here:
    Now 'tis so full that when therein I look
    I see a cloud of glory fills my book.
    Here stand it still to dignify our Muse,
    Your sober handmaid, who doth wisely choose
    Your name to be a laureate wreath to her
    Who doth both love and fear you, honoured sir.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To The High And Noble Prince George, Duke, Marquis, And Earl Of Buckingham."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
